housing.summary: Limited direct discussion of on-campus housing appears in these posts. Off-campus housing near Clemson (Central area) seems available around $625-800/month based on sublease discussions. RCM (Resident Community Manager) training exists as a structured two-week program before move-in.
key_quotes: We may have room at our son's place... $625 a month at Woodlands
Me and my current roommate are going abroad next spring and need a lease for this fall... not interested in any leases more than $800 a month
I will be an RCM next semester... two weeks of training that starts at 8am everyday

social_life.summary: Clemson is consistently described as having a strong, beloved campus culture and community feel. Students repeatedly emphasize it as a place people deeply love, with strong school spirit and a tight-knit atmosphere. The football culture is significant, and students describe it as a genuine 'home.'
key_quotes: Clemson is truly an amazing place and I hope everyone gets to experience having a home like this in their lifetime
Pickup basketball games at the court across from old stone church cemetery very regularly
my ap gov teacher went there and always talked about how much she loved it

dining.summary: There is virtually no discussion of dining halls or campus food in these posts. This is a significant data gap that makes it impossible to draw conclusions about food quality, variety, or dining plan value at Clemson.
key_quotes: No direct quotes available regarding dining
No student reviews of campus food found in dataset
No mentions of meal plans or dining halls

mental_health.summary: Mental health resources at Clemson are not directly discussed in these posts. One student mentions graduating high school depressed and unmotivated before eventually succeeding at a university. The competitive pre-med environment and heavy course loads are implied stressors.
key_quotes: This year was a mix of late nights, random memories, stressful moments, great people, and a lot of lessons learned
It somehow made another stressful semester feel worth it in the end
As someone who is done with Clemson (recently) all I have to say is: Good luck

financial_aid.summary: Clemson offers significant merit scholarships, including named full-ride scholarships like the Clemson National Scholars Program, which is highly regarded especially for pre-med students. In-state tuition is described as very affordable, making it a strong value proposition for SC residents. Scholarship deadlines are noted as important to watch.
key_quotes: If it's the Clemson National Scholars Program, I would take that every single time, and twice on Sundays. Every pre-med Clemson National Scholar got into medical school
Clemson. Come on. Why is this even a question? [regarding full ride vs UVA full pay]
Apparently they send out acceptance letters to students in November if they will receive scholarships

academics.summary: Clemson is consistently praised for engineering, particularly mechanical and electrical engineering, and is viewed as comparable to peer institutions like NC State. The pre-med track through the National Scholars Program has an exceptional reputation. Music programs are noted as weaker compared to USC, and neuroscience is not available as a major.
key_quotes: USC has a decent engineering program, but from what I've heard, Clemson's is better
As an engineering manager, I've hired from both schools. Equally good schools
If music is important to you, then USC is the clear choice. USC's music programs are at a much higher level than Clemson's

administration.summary: Limited direct commentary on Clemson's administration appears in these posts. The admissions process is described as having rolling/EA decisions with scholarship notifications in November. One frustrated comment from a recent graduate — 'As someone who is done with Clemson (recently) all I have to say is: Good luck' — hints at potential administrative frustrations without elaboration.
key_quotes: As someone who is done with Clemson (recently) all I have to say is: Good luck
I submitted October 14, and the letter is dated November 1 [regarding scholarship notification]
I just got off the waitlist yesterday and I'm a bit conflicted

location_and_campus.summary: Clemson's campus and location are spoken of with strong affection by students and alumni. It is situated in a smaller town environment (Central/Clemson area) in upstate South Carolina. The campus culture creates a strong sense of place and belonging that students consistently highlight.
key_quotes: Clemson is truly an amazing place and I hope everyone gets to experience having a home like this in their lifetime
Games would take place at Fike unless someone has a different location
There seems to be pickup games at the court across from old stone church cemetery very regularly

career_outcomes.summary: Engineering graduates from Clemson are well-regarded by employers, with hiring managers treating Clemson and NC State as equally strong. The National Scholars pre-med track reportedly has a near-perfect medical school acceptance rate. Finance and business career outcomes are less discussed.
key_quotes: As an engineering manager, I've hired from both schools. Equally good schools. Both schools are well known in engineering
Every pre-med Clemson National Scholar got into medical school
Getting a degree only helps you get in the door. After your first job, your experience matters far more than your degree

value_for_money.summary: For in-state students, Clemson is repeatedly described as an exceptional value, particularly with merit scholarships. Multiple advisors and students recommend Clemson over much more expensive out-of-state options like UVA. Out-of-state costs are noted as significantly higher and less competitive without scholarships.
key_quotes: Clemson in-state full ride [vs UVA full pay ~$90k/year]... Clemson. Come on. Why is this even a question?
Clemson's diversity is absolutely trash [but] I'm a SC resident and can afford Clemson for relatively cheap
Cost is the decisive factor in this ranking: Clemson 1, UNC 2, UVA 3

overall_vibe.summary: Clemson has a reputation as a school with exceptional community spirit and loyalty among its students and alumni. It is viewed as a strong public university with real academic substance, particularly in engineering and sciences, while maintaining a fun, spirited campus culture. The primary criticisms are around diversity and the lack of certain specialized programs.
key_quotes: Clemson is truly an amazing place and I hope everyone gets to experience having a home like this in their lifetime
Clemson's diversity is absolutely trash
My ap gov teacher went there and always talked about how much she loved it

red_flags.summary: The most consistently noted red flag is Clemson's lack of diversity, with one student bluntly stating 'Clemson's diversity is absolutely trash.' The university also lacks certain programs like neuroscience and has weaker arts/music offerings. A cryptic comment from a recent graduate saying only 'Good luck' raises questions about undisclosed issues with the student experience.
key_quotes: Clemson's diversity is absolutely trash
Clemson does not have [neuroscience as a major]
As someone who is done with Clemson (recently) all I have to say is: Good luck

hidden_gems.summary: The Clemson National Scholars Program is a standout hidden gem — a named full-ride scholarship with exceptional pre-med outcomes that few applicants know about. Fike Recreation Center is mentioned as a key student facility. The strong engineering placement with regional employers, comparable to much more expensive or harder-to-get-into schools, is an underappreciated advantage.
key_quotes: If it's the Clemson National Scholars Program, I would take that every single time, and twice on Sundays. Every pre-med Clemson National Scholar got into medical school
The perk of your special named full ride (assuming it's like research advantage) will help you get into medical school
Games would take place at Fike [Recreation Center]
